content.full_text:
September 10, 1990

Ms. Sandra Taylor  
Technical Assistance and Analysis Division  
Fair Political Practices Commission  
428 J Street, Suite 800  
Sacramento, California 95814

Dear Ms. Taylor:

Pursuant to telephone conversations between you and Walt Mix of our office on September 5, 1990, as well as my conversations with various FPPC consultants on September 6, 1990, please confirm that this letter accurately sets forth the Commission's advice on the following issues.

In an independent expenditure mass mailing which contains a letter, brochure, two response devices and a bumper sticker, the following placement of the "Notice to Voters" Statement complies with § 85600 of the Government Code.

* The envelope contains the disclaimer.
* The reverse side of a one-page, two-sided letter contains the disclaimer.
* An 8-1/2" x 11" brochure folded into thirds contains three disclaimers, one on each fold.
* The two response devices do not contain disclaimers.
* The bumper sticker does not contain a disclaimer.

The disclaimer is the one set forth in § 85600(a) of the Government Code, is in at least 10-point type, is not subjected to the half-tone or screening process, and is in a printed or drawn box set apart from any other printed matter.

If you have any questions or require any additional information, please feel free to contact me.

Very truly yours,

Bradley W. Hertz
